Stroller parking is extremely limited.

events.notes: Runs until 12 PM. For more info email Perla at perladelossantos@parentservices.org. Learning Bus visits every other Tuesday 10:30–11:15 AM.

events.notes: Outdoor on Town Plaza or Library Lawn. Moves to Council Chambers in inclement weather.

events.description: A Family Nature Day honoring Elizabeth Terwilliger at Richardson Bay Audubon in Tiburon. Join WildCare in partnership with Richardson Bay Audubon for nature activities, wildlife education, and a picnic overlooking Richardson Bay. Free street parking available.
events.notes: Bring a picnic to enjoy overlooking Richardson Bay. Free street parking.

events.description: Free community event for all ages with informative booths, fun activities, art, music, dancing, games, and talks. Visit 50+ exhibits from green businesses and environmental groups. Learn about clean energy, healthy food, resilient communities, and more.
events.notes: Runs until 3 PM. Sponsored by City of Mill Valley, County of Marin, MCE, and Sustainable Marin.

events.address: 118 E. Strawberry Drive, Mill Valley, CA 94941

events.notes: Runs until 10 PM. Food vendor on site. Bring blankets and chairs. Registration required — free.

events.description: A special Hawaiian hula and musical performance at the Mill Valley Library. A wonderful cultural experience for the whole family. Free and open to all.
events.notes: Runs until 11:30 AM. Registration recommended.

events.address: 118 E. Strawberry Drive, Mill Valley, CA 94941

events.venue: Old Mill Park → Downtown Mill Valley
events.event_name: Mill Valley Memorial Day Parade & Pancake Breakfast
events.address: Old Mill Park, 375 Throckmorton Ave, Mill Valley, CA 94941
events.description: Mill Valley's beloved annual Memorial Day celebration. Volunteer Firefighters Pancake Breakfast at City Hall 7–11 AM. Parade starts at Old Mill Park at 10:30 AM with floats, marching bands, dogs, clowns, and dancers winding through downtown Miller Ave.
events.notes: Pancake Breakfast 7–11 AM at City Hall. Parade 10:30 AM from Old Mill Park through downtown.

events.description: Family-focused summer street festival on Miller Avenue. Outdoor dining from local restaurants, kids crafts, bounce house, pop-up market with Mill Valley merchants, DJ, and community connection. Free and open to all.
events.notes: Approximately 500 families attend each night.

events.description: Family-focused summer street festival on Miller Avenue. Outdoor dining from local restaurants, kids crafts, bounce house, pop-up market with Mill Valley merchants, DJ, and community connection. Free and open to all.
events.notes: Approximately 500 families attend each night.

events.address: Mill Valley Downtown Plaza, Mill Valley, CA 94941
events.description: Annual Juneteenth Freedom Festival celebrating African American history and culture. Live entertainment, local food vendors, arts and crafts, interactive activities for kids, community vendors, and local organizations. Runs until 3 PM.
events.notes: Juneteenth commemorates June 19, 1865. Free and open to all.

events.description: Annual family campout at the Mill Valley Community Center! Fee includes camping space, dinner and breakfast, family games and activities, campfire entertainment, and preparedness activities. Children 3 and under free.
events.notes: Children 3 & under free. Register at MyMVR.org. Date estimated — confirm at cityofmillvalley.gov.

events.description: Monthly community celebration on the Sausalito waterfront. Live music, local art displays, family-friendly fun, sidewalk sales, restaurant specials, and a festive atmosphere exploring local shops and galleries. Encourages exploring Sausalito's creative character.
events.notes: Every 4th Saturday. 2026 dates: May 23, Jun 27, Jul 25, Aug 22, Sep 26, Oct 24, Nov 28, Dec 26.

events.address: 10 Avenue of the Flags, San Rafael, CA 94903
events.description: Marin's beloved annual county fair! Rides, live entertainment, farm animals, food, and Junior Division competitions for kids ages 5-18 including arts and crafts, photography, baking, and livestock. 2026 theme: Stars, Stripes & Stories. Entry deadline June 17.
events.registration: Not required for attending. Junior competition entry at marin.fairwire.com (small fee).
events.notes: July 1–5, 2026. Junior Division entry deadline June 17. Entry fees $2–$6 for competitions.

events.address: Downtown Mill Valley Plaza, Mill Valley, CA 94941
events.description: Mill Valley's biggest annual holiday celebration with approximately 2,500 attendees! Electric train for kids, live holiday performances, festive activities, and community spirit. A beloved Mill Valley tradition.
events.notes: 32nd Annual Winterfest. Check enjoymillvalley.com for exact times closer to date.

events.description: Intimate and festive holiday gingerbread house decorating experience for families. Sold out in its debut season — register early! Hosted by the Mill Valley Chamber of Commerce.
events.notes: Sold out in debut season — register early. Venue and time TBD — check website closer to date.

events.address: Bear Valley Visitor Center, 1 Bear Valley Rd, Point Reyes Station, CA 94956
events.description: Annual celebration of birds and wildlife at Point Reyes National Seashore. Guided birding walks, nature activities, wildlife presentations, and family-friendly programs all weekend. A wonderful introduction to West Marin's incredible biodiversity.
events.registration: Not required for most events — check pointreyesbirdingfestival.org
events.notes: Multi-day festival Fri–Sun. Various locations throughout Point Reyes. Some walks require registration.

events.description: A day-long celebration at Slide Ranch's beautiful coastal farm. Animal encounters, live music, nature exploration, cooking demonstrations, and more. A magical family experience on the Marin coast. Runs until 3 PM.
events.notes: Annual spring event. Check slideranch.org for tickets and details.

events.description: Free tours of historic tall ships at the Sausalito waterfront. Live entertainment, family activities, and a chance to explore beautiful sailing vessels. A wonderful maritime experience for kids and families. Runs until 2 PM.
events.notes: Annual spring event. Runs 10 AM–2 PM.

events.description: Family-friendly Earth Day festival at Tam Valley Community Center. Green activities, environmental exhibits, music, and community fun celebrating our planet. Runs until 3 PM.
events.notes: Annual Earth Day event. Runs 10 AM–3 PM.

events.event_name: Earth Day Celebration with Rescued Animals
events.description: Spend the day with rescued farm animals at Rancho Compasion in Nicasio. Live music with Charity and the JAMBand, animal encounters, and Earth Day celebration. A truly special West Marin experience for families. Runs until 1 PM.
events.notes: Annual Earth Day event. Register in advance.

events.description: A family day at the Presidio Riding Club Stables in the Marin Headlands. Meet the horses, watch a roping demonstration at 11 AM, enjoy arts and crafts and a bake sale. A fun unique experience for horse-loving families. Runs until 1:30 PM.
events.notes: Annual spring event in the Marin Headlands.

events.description: Annual community carnival bringing together every school in San Rafael. Games, activities, food, and family fun celebrating all that is great about the community. Benefits HeadsUp educational programs for San Rafael students. Runs until 3 PM.
events.notes: Annual spring community event.

events.event_name: Discovery Hike with WildCare Terwilliger Nature Guide
events.description: Immerse yourself in nature at Olompali with a discovery hike led by a WildCare Terwilliger Nature Guide. Learn about local wildlife, plants, and the natural history of this beautiful Marin park. Runs until noon.
events.notes: Part of the Elizabeth Terwilliger Nature Education legacy.

events.venue: Osher Marin JCC — Outdoor Tot Pool
events.address: 200 N. San Pedro Road, San Rafael, CA 94903
events.description: Monthly drop-in pool party in the JCC's outdoor warm-water toddler pool — just one foot deep and perfect for little ones. Includes age-appropriate pool toys, tunes, towels, and a bounce house. Family lane open in the big pool for older siblings. Bring a picnic dinner! Members free. Non-members $15 for one-child family, $20 for two or more children.
events.registration: Not required — pay at the door
events.notes: Seasonal May–October only. Monthly — check marinjcc.org for specific Friday dates each month. Members free.
